lines.gurmukhi: ਲਿਖਿਆ ਹੋਵੈ ਨਾਨਕਾ ਕਰਤਾ ਕਰੇ ਸੁ ਹੋਇ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: likhiaa hovai naanakaa karataa kare su hoe |1|
lines.english: Whatever is predestined, happens, O Nanak; whatever the Creator does, comes to pass. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਰੰਨਾ ਹੋਈਆ ਬੋਧੀਆ ਪੁਰਸ ਹੋਏ ਸਈਆਦ ॥
lines.translit: ranaa hoeea bodheea puras hoe seeaad |
lines.english: Women have become advisors, and men have become hunters.

lines.translit: mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਸਚਾ ਏਕੁ ਹੈ ਅਉਰੁ ਨ ਸਚਾ ਭਾਲਿ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: naanak sachaa ek hai aaur na sachaa bhaal |2|
lines.english: O Nanak, there is only One True Lord; do not bother to search for any other as true. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਰਮੁ ਗਇਆ ਘਰਿ ਆਪਣੈ ਪਤਿ ਉਠਿ ਚਲੀ ਨਾਲਿ ॥
lines.translit: saram geaa ghar aapanai pat utth chalee naal |
lines.english: Modesty has left her home, and honor has gone away with her.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੀਲੁ ਸੰਜਮੁ ਸੁਚ ਭੰਨੀ ਖਾਣਾ ਖਾਜੁ ਅਹਾਜੁ ॥
lines.translit: seel sanjam such bhanee khaanaa khaaj ahaaj |
lines.english: Humility, self-control and purity have run away; people eat the uneatable, forbidden food.

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਖਿੰਥਾ ਝੋਲੀ ਬਹੁ ਭੇਖ ਕਰੇ ਦੁਰਮਤਿ ਅਹੰਕਾਰੀ ॥
lines.translit: khinthaa jholee bahu bhekh kare duramat ahankaaree |
lines.english: You wear the patched coat and all the right clothes and robes, but you are still egotistical and proud.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਾਹਰਿ ਭਸਮ ਲੇਪਨ ਕਰੇ ਅੰਤਰਿ ਗੁਬਾਰੀ ॥
lines.translit: baahar bhasam lepan kare antar gubaaree |
lines.english: You smear your outer body with ashes, but within, you are filled with darkness.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਹਿਬ ਸਬਦੁ ਨ ਊਚਰੈ ਮਾਇਆ ਮੋਹ ਪਸਾਰੀ ॥
lines.translit: saahib sabad na aoocharai maaeaa moh pasaaree |
lines.english: You do not chant the Shabad, the Word of Your Lord and Master; you are attached to the expanse of Maya.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਤਰਿ ਲਾਲਚੁ ਭਰਮੁ ਹੈ ਭਰਮੈ ਗਾਵਾਰੀ ॥
lines.translit: antar laalach bharam hai bharamai gaavaaree |
lines.english: Within, you are filled with greed and doubt; you wander around like a fool.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਨਾਮੁ ਨ ਚੇਤਈ ਜੂਐ ਬਾਜੀ ਹਾਰੀ ॥੧੪॥
lines.translit: naanak naam na chetee jooai baajee haaree |14|
lines.english: Says Nanak, you never even think of the Naam; you have lost the game of life in the gamble. ||14||

lines.gurmukhi: ਲਖ ਸਿਉ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ਹੋਵੈ ਲਖ ਜੀਵਣੁ ਕਿਆ ਖੁਸੀਆ ਕਿਆ ਚਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: lakh siau preet hovai lakh jeevan kiaa khuseea kiaa chaau |
lines.english: You may be in love with tens of thousands, and live for thousands of years; but what good are these pleasures and occupations?

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕ ਮਃ ੧ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: Salok, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇ ਸਉ ਵਰ੍ਹਿਆ ਮਿਠਾ ਖਾਜੈ ਭੀ ਫਿਰਿ ਕਉੜਾ ਖਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: je sau varhiaa mitthaa khaajai bhee fir kaurraa khaae |
lines.english: You may eat sweets for a hundred years, but eventually, you will have to eat the bitter as well.

lines.gurmukhi: ਵਿਛੁੜਿਆ ਵਿਸੁ ਹੋਇ ਵਿਛੋੜਾ ਏਕ ਘੜੀ ਮਹਿ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: vichhurriaa vis hoe vichhorraa ek gharree meh jaae |
lines.english: And when you must separate from them, that separation is like poison, but they will be gone in an instant.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਿਠਾ ਖਾਧਾ ਚਿਤਿ ਨ ਆਵੈ ਕਉੜਤਣੁ ਧਾਇ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: mitthaa khaadhaa chit na aavai kaurratan dhaae jaae |
lines.english: Then, you will not remember eating the sweets; bitterness will permeate you.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਿਠਾ ਕਉੜਾ ਦੋਵੈ ਰੋਗ ॥
lines.translit: mitthaa kaurraa dovai rog |
lines.english: The sweet and the bitter are both diseases.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਅੰਤਿ ਵਿਗੁਤੇ ਭੋਗ ॥
lines.translit: naanak ant vigute bhog |
lines.english: O Nanak, eating them, you will come to ruin in the end.

lines.gurmukhi: ਝਖਿ ਝਖਿ ਝਖਣਾ ਝਗੜਾ ਝਾਖ ॥
lines.translit: jhakh jhakh jhakhanaa jhagarraa jhaakh |
lines.english: It is useless to worry and struggle to death.

lines.gurmukhi: ਝਖਿ ਝਖਿ ਜਾਹਿ ਝਖਹਿ ਤਿਨੑ ਪਾਸਿ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: jhakh jhakh jaeh jhakheh tina paas |1|
lines.english: Entangled in worries and struggles, people exhaust themselves. ||1||

lines.translit: mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਾਪੜੁ ਕਾਠੁ ਰੰਗਾਇਆ ਰਾਂਗਿ ॥
lines.translit: kaaparr kaatth rangaaeaa raang |
lines.english: They have fine clothes and furniture of various colors.

lines.gurmukhi: ਘਰ ਗਚ ਕੀਤੇ ਬਾਗੇ ਬਾਗ ॥
lines.translit: ghar gach keete baage baag |
lines.english: Their houses are painted beautifully white.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਦ ਸਹਜ ਕਰਿ ਮਨੁ ਖੇਲਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: saad sehaj kar man khelaaeaa |
lines.english: In pleasure and poise, they play their mind games.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੈ ਸਹ ਪਾਸਹੁ ਕਹਣੁ ਕਹਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: tai seh paasahu kehan kahaaeaa |
lines.english: When they approach You, O Lord, they shall be spoken to.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਿਨਿ ਕਉੜੈ ਤਨਿ ਰੋਗੁ ਜਮਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: tin kaurrai tan rog jamaaeaa |
lines.english: The bitter disease grows in the body.

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਿਠਾ ਕਰਿ ਕੈ ਕਉੜਾ ਖਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: mitthaa kar kai kaurraa khaaeaa |
lines.english: They think it is sweet, so they eat the bitter.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਉ ਕਉੜਤਣੁ ਚੂਕਸਿ ਮਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: tau kaurratan chookas maae |
lines.english: then their bitterness shall be gone, O mother.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੇ ਫਿਰਿ ਮਿਠਾ ਪੇੜੈ ਪਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: je fir mitthaa perrai paae |
lines.english: If, later on, they receive the sweet,

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਪਾਵੈ ਸੋਇ ॥
lines.translit: naanak guramukh paavai soe |
lines.english: O Nanak, the Gurmukh is blessed to receive

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਸ ਨੋ ਪ੍ਰਾਪਤਿ ਲਿਖਿਆ ਹੋਇ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: jis no praapat likhiaa hoe |2|
lines.english: what he is predestined to receive. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਉੜੀ ॥
lines.translit: paurree |
lines.english: Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਨ ਕੈ ਹਿਰਦੈ ਮੈਲੁ ਕਪਟੁ ਹੈ ਬਾਹਰੁ ਧੋਵਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: jin kai hiradai mail kapatt hai baahar dhovaaeaa |
lines.english: Those whose hearts are filled with the filth of deception, may wash themselves on the outside.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੂੜੁ ਕਪਟੁ ਕਮਾਵਦੇ ਕੂੜੁ ਪਰਗਟੀ ਆਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: koorr kapatt kamaavade koorr paragattee aaeaa |
lines.english: They practice falsehood and deception, and their falsehood is revealed.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੂੜੈ ਲਾਲਚਿ ਲਗਿਆ ਫਿਰਿ ਜੂਨੀ ਪਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: koorrai laalach lagiaa fir joonee paaeaa |
lines.english: Attached to falsehood and greed, the mortal is consigned to reincarnation over and over again.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਦਰਿ ਹੋਇ ਸੁ ਨਿਕਲੈ ਨਹ ਛਪੈ ਛਪਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: andar hoe su nikalai neh chhapai chhapaaeaa |
lines.english: That which is within them, comes out; it cannot be concealed by concealment.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਲੋਕ ਮਃ ੨ ॥
lines.translit: salok mahalaa 2 |
lines.english: Salok, Second Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਜੋ ਬੀਜੈ ਸੋ ਖਾਵਣਾ ਕਰਤੈ ਲਿਖਿ ਪਾਇਆ ॥੧੫॥
lines.translit: naanak jo beejai so khaavanaa karatai likh paaeaa |15|
lines.english: O Nanak, whatever the mortal plants, he must eat. The Creator Lord has written our destiny. ||15||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਥਾ ਕਹਾਣੀ ਬੇਦਂ‍ੀ ਆਣੀ ਪਾਪੁ ਪੁੰਨੁ ਬੀਚਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: kathaa kahaanee bedanee aanee paap pun beechaar |
lines.english: The Vedas bring forth stories and legends, and thoughts of vice and virtue.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦੇ ਦੇ ਲੈਣਾ ਲੈ ਲੈ ਦੇਣਾ ਨਰਕਿ ਸੁਰਗਿ ਅਵਤਾਰ ॥
lines.translit: de de lainaa lai lai denaa narak surag avataar |
lines.english: What is given, they receive, and what is received, they give. They are reincarnated in heaven and hell.

lines.gurmukhi: ਉਤਮ ਮਧਿਮ ਜਾਤੀਂ ਜਿਨਸੀ ਭਰਮਿ ਭਵੈ ਸੰਸਾਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: autam madhim jaateen jinasee bharam bhavai sansaar |
lines.english: High and low, social class and status - the world wanders lost in superstition.

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਬਾਣੀ ਤਤੁ ਵਖਾਣੀ ਗਿਆਨ ਧਿਆਨ ਵਿਚਿ ਆਈ ॥
lines.translit: amrit baanee tat vakhaanee giaan dhiaan vich aaee |
lines.english: The Ambrosial Word of Gurbani proclaims the essence of reality. Spiritual wisdom and meditation are contained within it.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹੁਕਮੁ ਸਾਜਿ ਹੁਕਮੈ ਵਿਚਿ ਰਖੈ ਹੁਕਮੈ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਵੇਖੈ ॥
lines.translit: hukam saaj hukamai vich rakhai hukamai andar vekhai |
lines.english: By the Hukam of His Command, He formed the Universe, and in His Hukam, He keeps it. By His Hukam, He keeps it under His Gaze.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਆਖੀ ਗੁਰਮੁਖਿ ਜਾਤੀ ਸੁਰਤਂ‍ੀ ਕਰਮਿ ਧਿਆਈ ॥
lines.translit: guramukh aakhee guramukh jaatee suratanee karam dhiaaee |
lines.english: The Gurmukhs chant it, and the Gurmukhs realize it. Intuitively aware, they meditate on it.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਅਗਹੁ ਹਉਮੈ ਤੁਟੈ ਤਾਂ ਕੋ ਲਿਖੀਐ ਲੇਖੈ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: naanak agahu haumai tuttai taan ko likheeai lekhai |1|
lines.english: O Nanak, if the mortal shatters his ego before he departs, as it is pre-ordained, then he is approved. ||1||

lines.translit: mahalaa 1 |
lines.english: First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਬੇਦੁ ਪੁਕਾਰੇ ਪੁੰਨੁ ਪਾਪੁ ਸੁਰਗ ਨਰਕ ਕਾ ਬੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: bed pukaare pun paap surag narak kaa beeo |
lines.english: The Vedas proclaim that vice and virtue are the seeds of heaven and hell.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਚੁ ਬੀਜੈ ਸਚੁ ਉਗਵੈ ਦਰਗਹ ਪਾਈਐ ਥਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: sach beejai sach ugavai daragah paaeeai thaau |
lines.english: When Truth is planted, Truth grows. In the Court of the Lord, you shall find your place of honor.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗਿਆਨੁ ਸਲਾਹੇ ਵਡਾ ਕਰਿ ਸਚੋ ਸਚਾ ਨਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: giaan salaahe vaddaa kar sacho sachaa naau |
lines.english: Whoever praises spiritual wisdom as great, becomes truthful in the True Name.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜੋ ਬੀਜੈ ਸੋ ਉਗਵੈ ਖਾਂਦਾ ਜਾਣੈ ਜੀਉ ॥
lines.translit: jo beejai so ugavai khaandaa jaanai jeeo |
lines.english: Whatever is planted, shall grow. The soul eats the fruits of its actions, and understands.
